topic: Create and manage Azure Container Registry (ACR)
key_facts: Azure Container Registry transfers container images over HTTPS, and it supports TLS to help secure client connections.
Security features of the Premium service tier include content trust for image tag signing, and private endpoints (preview) to restrict access to the registry.
Azure provides tooling like the Azure CLI, the Azure portal, and API support to manage your container registries.
Each tier supports common features such as webhook integration, registry authentication with Microsoft Entra ID, and delete functionality.
Premium registries support additional capabilities and have greater storage and limits.

Supported images and artifacts

 When images are grouped in a repository, each image is a read-only snapshot of a Docker-compatible container.

Container registry security and access

 You authenticate to a registry by using the Azure CLI or the standard docker login command.
